text:
[residual] If a future surface gated these fields behind auth, an auth-path inconsistency would go unswept — no such path today.
[residual] If question visibility became role/tenant-scoped, mis-scoped access could go unswept — no such model now.
[residual] Briefing not captured in recorded plan dialog could reduce traceability of why a question was asked — otherwise under observability.
[residual] If critical-question reviewers were spawned in parallel over shared interview-state, a write-ordering clobber could occur — but reviewers are read-only over question text.
[residual] Reviewer/generator passes depend on LLM-host availability; if host absent the pure gate still runs but the fresh-context reviewer is silently skipped.
[residual] Per-critical-question reviewer multiplies LLM token cost; an unbounded regenerate-on-reject loop could spin — covered under failure-recovery; not capacity collapse.
[residual] If candidates were ever sourced from untrusted external input, the gate has no abuse/quota defense — not the case today.
[residual] Question text flows into context-reviewer LLM prompt — a soft prompt-injection path if a candidate were adversarial, but candidates are locally generated.
[residual] grounding/background embed codebase excerpts; a future change shipping that state off-box (logs, error reports, accidentally tracking .ditto/local) could surface a secret literal.
[residual] A grounding excerpt could quote developer intent text containing PII into local state — no masking, but no PII collection designed.
[residual] None material — no regulated-data flow introduced or touched.
[residual] An interview begun under old schema then continued under rebuilt binary could mis-parse new required fields — swept under compat-version + data-integrity.
[residual] required-vs-optional field policy acts like an implicit default; if too lax it weakens enforcement — covered under minimal-increment; no new config knob.
[residual] None material — no TTL, certificate, or wall-vs-monotonic clock logic introduced.
[close_reason] Local single-user dev CLI; no authentication path. No auth/oauth/jwt/cookie/session code in scoped files. Change validates question-text fields and renders them.
[close_reason] No authorization model (RBAC/ABAC/ACL/tenant/ownership); questions shown to single local owner. Gate grants/requires no permissions.
[close_reason] Single-user local tool, no repudiation/tamper threat model. serializePlanDialog is traceability not audit log.
[close_reason] validateQuestionContext pure/sync; state single-process single-writer JSON; reviewers spawned per-question sequentially. No new async/lock/shared mutable state.
[close_reason] No external API/3rd-party/network call (no fetch/http/socket). Side effects are local JSON writes + spawning local agents via host.
[close_reason] Bounded interview-sized question count, no DB/N+1/memory growth, single local user. Gate is O(1) per candidate.
[close_reason] Candidates produced by trusted local agent, not untrusted external input; no external flow, rate limiter, replay/scraping surface.
[close_reason] No dangerous interpreter sink (no SQL/NoSQL/OS-cmd/LDAP/XPath/template-eval/deserialization). Question text rendered as display/markdown, persisted as JSON string, input trusted-local.
[close_reason] No secret/credential/token/key handled. New background/grounding fields copy codebase excerpts into displayed text + gitignored .ditto/local state shown to local owner who already holds the codebase.
[close_reason] Dev tool whose questions concern code/intent not personal data; no PII collected/logged/transmitted; state gitignored local JSON.
[close_reason] No personal-data processing/consent/retention/cross-border surface in local single-user dev CLI.
[close_reason] Single self-hosted binary (dogfood=rebuild bin/ditto); no multi-service deploy ordering, no DB migration, no feature flag/release train.
[close_reason] No new env var/flag/default config; gate is code (zod + pure
